title: Write a Custom Falco Rule
description:
## Write a Custom Falco Rule

Falco allows security administrators to write custom rules to detect domain-specific threats. In this task, you will create a new custom rule to monitor access to `/etc/shadow` files inside containers.

**Your task:**

Create a Kubernetes ConfigMap named `falco-custom-rules` in the namespace `falco`. The ConfigMap must contain a key named `custom-rules.yaml` storing the rule definition in Falco's standard YAML list format. The rule must possess:
- **`rule`**: `Read Sensitive File`
- **`desc`**: `Detects attempts to read sensitive system file /etc/shadow`
- **`condition`**: `evt.type = open and fd.name = "/etc/shadow"` (or matching logic targeting `/etc/shadow` open events)
- **`output`**: `Sensitive file read (user=%user.name command=%proc.cmdline file=%fd.name)`
- **`priority`**: `WARNING`

```yaml
# Expected structure inside custom-rules.yaml:
- rule: Read Sensitive File
  desc: ...
  condition: ...
  output: ...
  priority: ...
```

hints.title: Creating the ConfigMap
hints.body: Use a manifest file or standard heredoc to create the ConfigMap. Remember, Falco rules are always lists of rules/macros/lists in YAML.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: falco-custom-rules
  namespace: falco
data:
  custom-rules.yaml: |
    - rule: Read Sensitive File
      desc: Detects attempts to read sensitive system file /etc/shadow
      condition: evt.type = open and fd.name = "/etc/shadow"
      output: Sensitive file read (user=%user.name command=%proc.cmdline file=%fd.name)
      priority: WARNING
EOF

commands.description: ConfigMap falco-custom-rules exists in namespace falco
commands.command: kubectl get configmap falco-custom-rules -n falco -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'

commands.description: ConfigMap contains the custom rule with correct condition and priority
commands.command: python3 -c 'import yaml, subprocess; out = subprocess.check_output(["kubectl", "get", "configmap", "falco-custom-rules", "-n", "falco", "-o", "jsonpath={.data.custom-rules\\.yaml}"]); data = yaml.safe_load(out); rules = [x for x in data if isinstance(x, dict) and x.get("rule") == "Read Sensitive File"]; print("ok" if rules and "/etc/shadow" in rules[0].get("condition", "") and rules[0].get("priority") == "WARNING" else "fail")'
